prs.title: fix: support Ctrl+Shift keyboard shortcuts for text direction in RTL documents
prs.body:
#2382 
# Fix: Support Ctrl+Shift keyboard shortcuts for text direction in RTL documents

## Overview

This PR adds support for Windows standard keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift) to properly switch text direction in Slidev's integrated editor when working with RTL documents.

## Problem

When editing RTL presentations (e.g., Hebrew with `htmlAttrs: { dir: rtl }`), pressing Ctrl+Left Shift or Ctrl+Right Shift would cause the browser to internally switch text direction, but the visual rendering would break because the `dir` attribute wasn't being synchronized on the textarea element.

This made it difficult to edit mixed-direction content (e.g., inserting English code or formulas in an RTL Hebrew presentation).

## Solution

Added a new `useTextDirection` composable that:
- Listens for Ctrl+Shift keyboard combinations
- Detects which Shift key was pressed (left or right) using `KeyboardEvent.location`
- Sets the appropriate `dir` attribute on the textarea element
- Uses Vue's `watch` to ensure the event listener is attached when the element becomes available

## Changes

### New File
- `packages/client/composables/useTextDirection.ts` - Composable for handling direction switching

### Modified Files
- `packages/client/internals/NoteEditable.vue` - Applied composable to notes editor
- `packages/client/internals/ShikiEditor.vue` - Applied composable to Shiki code editor

## Testing

Tested manually in presenter mode with an RTL document:

1. Set `htmlAttrs: { dir: rtl, lang: he }` in slides.md
2. Open presenter mode at http://localhost:3030/presenter/
3. Click in the notes editor
4. Press **Ctrl+Right Shift** → textarea gets `dir="rtl"` ✓
5. Press **Ctrl+Left Shift** → textarea gets `dir="ltr"` ✓

Text direction switches properly with correct visual rendering and caret positioning.

## Related Issues

Fixes the issue where Ctrl+Shift shortcuts don't work properly in RTL documents as described in the original bug report.

prs.title: improved the readme.md  and docker with followed instructions
prs.body:
I've made some updates to our Docker configuration and README to make local development smoother and more practical, especially when working with OAuth.

Here’s a quick rundown of what’s changed:

*Key Improvements*

--Integrated ngrok for Easy OAuth: I've added an ngrok service to the [docker-compose.yml](code-assist-path:/Users/priyabratasingh/Downloads/hacktoberfest25/hackathon-starter/docker-compose.yml). This automatically creates a secure public URL for our local instance, which is a huge help for testing OAuth providers that require an HTTPS callback URL. No more fighting with localtunnel or manual setups!
Persistent MongoDB Data: The MongoDB container now uses a named volume to store its data. This means you won't lose all your users and test data every time you run docker compose down.
Clear, Step-by-Step Instructions: I completely rewrote the Docker section in the [README.md](code-assist-path:/Users/priyabratasingh/Downloads/hacktoberfest25/hackathon-starter/README.md). The new guide walks you through the setup process step-by-step, from creating the .env file to getting the ngrok URL and starting the services in the right order.

*Why These Changes Matter*

--The previous Docker setup was a bit too basic and didn't work out-of-the-box for features that need a public endpoint (like most of our API integrations). These changes create a much more realistic and robust development environment that mirrors how we'd use the app in production.

Plus, the new documentation should make it way easier for new contributors (and us!) to get up and running quickly.

Let me know what you think

prs.title: Optimize Queue Class: Cleaner Methods & Functional Dequeue
prs.body:
### 📋 **PR Details**

**Description of Changes:**  
_Improved the existing queue implementation using a linked list._  
- Added return values for `dequeue()` to make it more functional.  
- Simplified `is_empty()` for clarity.  
- Added `__str__()` to visualize the queue contents easily.  
- Improved code readability and added type hints for better maintainability.  
- Maintains O(1) time complexity for enqueue and dequeue operations.

**Related Issues/Discussions:**  
_None directly, this is a general improvement to the queue implementation._

---

### 📝 **Contributor Info**

**Your Name:**  
_Priyabrata Singh_

---

### ✅ **Checklist Before Submitting**

- [x] I have checked the code for errors and ensured it follows the project's guidelines.  
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines.  
- [x] I have participated in related discussions or created an issue if necessary.  
- [x] I have included my name above.  

---

### 📣 **Additional Comments**

_This PR makes the queue implementation more Pythonic, functional, and easier to use in educational or real-world scenarios. Feedback is welcome!_

---

prs.title: UI modify
prs.body:
I've implemented the two types of user registration in your Fix-Time application:
Backend Changes:
  Updated the User model to include a 'role' field with 'customer' or 'provider' options
  Added businessInfo fields for service providers (businessName, description, category, hours)
  Modified the auth controller to handle both customer and provider registration and include proper validation
  Frontend Changes:
  Enhanced the registration form with a user type selector (Customer/Service Provider)
  Added conditional business information fields for service providers
  Added validation for business fields
  Created appropriate styling for the new form elements

Features:
  Users can select between registering as a customer or service provider
  Service providers must enter business information (business name is required)
  Business categories are aligned with your existing service categories
  User roles are stored in the database and included in auth responses
  The UI is fully responsive and maintains your existing design language
  The system is now ready to distinguish between customers who book appointments and service providers who offer them. This will help you build different user experiences and permissions for each user type.

prs.title: UI modify
prs.body:
I've updated all the category pages to fetch services from the API instead of using static data. Now, all category pages follow the same consistent pattern:

Each category page now:

  Fetches services from the backend API using the appropriate category name
  Displays proper loading, error, and empty states
  Maps service names to appropriate icons using a category-specific mapping
  Refreshes the service list when a new service is added
  Uses the service's MongoDB ID as the key for React lists

Key improvements across all pages:

  Consistent category naming between frontend and backend
  Proper error handling with user-friendly messages
  Empty state handling when no services are available
  Real-time updates when new services are added

Added features:
  Each category has its own icon mapping to ensure services display with appropriate icons
  Default icons for any new services that don't match predefined names
  Automatic refresh when a service is added through the form

Now when users add a service through the form in any category, it will be properly stored in the database and immediately displayed on the correct category page. The implementation is consistent across all categories, ensuring a uniform user experience throughout the application.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Service categories now display live data fetched from the backend, replacing static mock data.
  * Service forms provide comprehensive client-side validation, improved error messages, and time slot management.
  * Error and empty state messages are visually enhanced across service-related pages.
  * Added a database seeding script to populate initial service data.

* **Improvements**
  * Enhanced mobile responsiveness and accessibility for service forms.
  * Service lists automatically refresh when new services are added.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Fixed category naming inconsistencies for beauty services.

* **Chores**
  * Added a script to seed the database with sample services.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: UI modify
prs.body: implemented a complete solution to update the calendar when appointments are rescheduled: We've enhanced the RescheduleAppointment component to: Store updated appointment data in localStorage Set a flag indicating an appointment was updated Navigate to the calendar with a query parameter We've updated the AppointmentCalendar component to: Check localStorage for updated appointments Check for the query parameter from the reschedule page Show a notification when appointments are updated Refresh the calendar display with the updated data We've added proper styling for the notification and buttons Now when a user reschedules an appointment: The appointment details are stored in localStorage When they navigate to the calendar, it will detect the changes The calendar will update with the rescheduled appointment A notification will appear confirming the update This provides a seamless user experience with immediate feedback when appointments are rescheduled.

prs.title: UI modification
prs.body:
I've successfully implemented several fixes to address the appointment fetching issues:
Environment Variables:
Created README files with instructions for setting up .env files in both client and server directories
Updated code to use environment variables with fallbacks for API URLs
MongoDB Compatibility:
Downgraded Mongoose from v8.15.2 to v6.9.0 for better compatibility with various MongoDB versions
API URL Configuration:
Added API_BASE_URL constant to replace hardcoded URLs
Updated all API endpoints to use this configurable base URL
Improved Error Handling:
Added better error logging and validation for appointment data
Added check for missing appointment IDs
Documentation:
Added a comprehensive troubleshooting section to the main README
Created detailed setup instructions for both client and server
All changes have been committed and pushed to the ui-modification branch. These changes should resolve the appointment fetching issues experienced by users who clone the repository.

prs.title: Review system added to backend + Frontend
prs.body:
Created a reusable ServiceCard component that displays service information along with ratings
Created a utility function addRatingsToServices to add ratings to all services
Updated all category components to use the new ServiceCard component and display ratings
Added a reviews section to the appointment booking page
Ensured consistent styling across all components
Now, all services across all categories will display ratings, and users can view detailed reviews before booking appointments. After completing appointments, they can submit their own reviews, which will update the service's average rating and total review count.
This implementation provides a complete and consistent user review system throughout the application, enhancing the user experience and helping others make informed decisions when booking services.
